site.name: oliverbrown.design
site.title: Oliver Brown — Teacher & Designer
site.description: Oliver Brown builds targeted EdTech that makes learners into doers and lets teachers be teachers.

about.heading: About
paragraphs: I'm a teacher who designs and builds educational technology. After years in the classroom, I started making the tools I always wished I had — software that targets the specific things learners struggle with, and gets out of the teacher's way.
I work across the whole stack: pedagogy, interaction design, and code. Each project starts from a real classroom problem and ends as something children actually want to use.
Step through the door and take a look around the classroom.

pieces.title: TREASURE BOX
pieces.blurb: Learners solve a huge variety of math tasks using an innovative place value modeling tool.
overview: Treasure Box reimagines place value as something you can pick up, group, and trade. Learners build numbers physically inside the app and solve a wide range of math tasks using a single, consistent mental model.
The tool grows with the learner — the same interface that introduces tens and ones scales up to decimals and operations, so the underlying idea never has to be re-taught.

pieces.title: MEMORY METRO
pieces.blurb: Unique math practice + working memory training game. Solo developed with Claude Code.
overview: Memory Metro blends math fluency practice with genuine working-memory training. Players route trains through a growing transit map while holding and updating numbers in their head — the difficulty adapts to keep each learner at the edge of their capacity.
Designed and built solo with Claude Code, from the game loop and difficulty model down to the art direction.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.blurb: Building something for learners or teachers? I'd love to hear about it.
